title: Proactive monitoring – COVID-19: Health product advertising incidents - Canada.ca
content:
## Health product advertising incidents: Proactive monitoring – COVID-19
- How to report a complaint
- Proactive monitoring – COVID-19
- Proactive monitoring – Natural health products
The World Health Organization (WHO) declared the outbreak of the novel coronavirus (COVID-19) a global pandemic in March 2020.
Health Canada began proactively monitoring online content, including websites, e-commerce platforms and social media. Our goal was to protect consumers from illegal, false or misleading advertising of health products claiming to mitigate, prevent, treat, diagnose or cure COVID-19.
We took the appropriate compliance and enforcement actions when incidents were identified. These incidents are now considered resolved.
The following table summarizes the non-compliant health product advertising incidents that we identified between March 27, 2020, and February 29, 2024. Including a product in this table does not mean the manufacturer was involved in the non-compliant advertising or that the advertising materials originated from the third-party seller.
If you have questions about the incidents in the summary table, email us at drug-device-marketing@hc-sc.gc.ca .
